name: l6e-budget
tagline: Per-session cost guardrails for AI coding agents
description:
l6e-budget gives your OpenClaw agent a hard budget ceiling and a blocking gate before expensive work. Before each stage transition or sub-agent launch, the agent calls `l6e_authorize_call` and gets back `allow`, `reroute`, or `halt` — keeping sessions from silently running up large bills.

This is the OSS estimate-first path: all accounting is based on token estimates the agent constructs before each call, using live pricing data from litellm. No proxy, no backend required.

Budget state persists in a local SQLite database (`~/.l6e/sessions.db`) and run summaries are flushed to a JSONL log at session end. Designed to work alongside OpenClaw's native Adaptive Model Routing — l6e enforces the cost ceiling while Adaptive Routing handles the local-first optimisation within it.
